title: Masking Strategies for Inpainting
concept: The shape and size of the mask significantly affects results; larger masks give the model more creative freedom but less guidance from context.

exercises.prompt: When you erase part of a photo and ask AI to fill it back in, what do you think the AI uses to decide what to draw?

options.text: It guesses randomly with no clues at all
It looks at the pixels around the erased spot for clues

exercises.explain: AI inpainting tools look at the surrounding, unmasked pixels as context clues to figure out what should go in the erased area.

cards.title: The Hole in the Photo
cards.body: Imagine erasing a small chunk of your friend's face vs. erasing their ENTIRE head. Which one do you think the AI can fix more accurately? The size and shape of that erased 'hole' — called a mask — changes everything about how the AI fills it in!

cards.title: What Is a Mask?
cards.body: A mask is the shape you draw over an image to tell the AI 'erase this part and fill it back in.' Small masks give the AI lots of surrounding context to copy from, so results look accurate. Large masks remove most of the clues, so the AI has to invent more on its own — giving it more creative freedom but less guidance.

cards.title: A Puzzle With Missing Pieces
cards.body: Think of a jigsaw puzzle. If only one small piece is missing, you can easily guess what goes there using the pieces around it. But if half the puzzle is missing, you're basically making up a whole new picture. Small mask = one missing piece. Big mask = half the puzzle gone!

cards.title: Removing a Bird vs. a Building
cards.body: If you mask out a tiny bird flying in the sky, the AI easily fills in more sky because the surrounding blue gives it clear clues. But if you mask out an entire building in a city photo, the AI must invent windows, doors, and shapes from scratch — the results are far less predictable.

cards.title: Myth: Bigger Mask Always Means Better Edit
cards.body: Some people think erasing a huge area gives the AI 'more room to work' and better results. Actually the opposite is often true! Bigger masks remove more helpful context, so the AI has to guess more, which can lead to weird, mismatched, or blurry results.

cards.title: Small Mask vs. Large Mask
cards.body: Compare how much guidance the AI gets based on mask size.

left.text: Lots of surrounding context → accurate, predictable fill-in

right.text: Little surrounding context → more creative, less predictable fill-in

exercises.prompt: Why does a larger mask give the AI more 'creative freedom' but less guidance?

options.text: Because larger masks cover more of the surrounding context clues
Because larger masks make the image file bigger
Because the AI ignores large masks completely
Because larger masks always use a different AI model

exercises.explain: A larger mask hides more of the original pixels, so the AI has fewer clues from context and must rely more on its own imagination.

exercises.prompt: A small mask usually gives the AI more context clues than a large mask.
exercises.explain: True! Small masks leave most of the original image visible, giving the AI plenty of surrounding detail to guide its fill-in.

exercises.sentence: A ____ mask removes more context, so the AI must invent more of the image on its own.
exercises.explain: A large mask hides more of the original image, removing helpful context and forcing the AI to rely more on creative guessing.

exercises.prompt: Which masking choice would most likely give the most predictable, accurate inpainting result?

options.text: Masking a small, tightly-drawn shape around just the object you want changed
Masking half of the entire image
Masking the whole image with no unmasked pixels left

exercises.explain: Keeping the mask small and tight around only the area you want changed preserves the most surrounding context, leading to more accurate, predictable results.

exercises.prompt: You want to remove just a small coffee stain from a photo of a desk, keeping everything else looking natural and accurate. What masking strategy should you use, and why?

options.text: Use a small mask tightly around the stain, so the AI has lots of surrounding desk texture to guide an accurate fill-in
Mask the entire desk so the AI can redraw everything fresh
Mask a random large rectangle anywhere on the photo
Skip masking and just ask the AI to imagine no stain exists

exercises.explain: Since you only want to fix a small imperfection, a small tight mask preserves the most context (desk texture, lighting, color) around the stain, giving the AI the best guidance for a seamless, accurate repair. Large or unnecessary masks would remove helpful context and risk mismatched results.
